title: Neptunia plena
uses: Neptunia plena, a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, has been studied for its potential medicinal uses. taxonomy: Neptunia plena is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus or thyme-leaf family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Neptunia plena is a member of the genus Neptunia, which consists of several species of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification is also influenced by its morphological features, such as its leaf shape and arrangement. Neptunia plena is typically classified as a perennial herbaceous plant, with a growth habit that is often spreading or mat-forming.
